subject: subtomogram averaging with warp extracted particles
body:
Hi all,

I'm looking at doing subtomogram averaging with particles extracted in warp 
and have a couple of questions

   1. In the designed workflow, (warp extraction, relion alignment, (later 
   M... :)) is there anything particular about the way I should run 
   relion_refine? I've tried with --ctf (in relion 3.0.8) but seem to be 
   getting stuck suspiciously close to the average first zero of the CTF. Do I 
   need any of the other ctf-related options enabled? (--ctf_phase_flipped 
   --ctf_multiplied etc). 
   2. I see from the paper that the weights are inserted as |CTF| and 
   weighted by the dose-dependent B-factor so assume that multiplication in 
   fourier space of the particle with the CTF volume brings them to CTF^2. Am 
   I right in thinking the particles themselves have already been phase 
   flipped? If so, I should be able to try subtomogram in other packages 
   directly on these files, correct?
   
Thanks in advance!

Best,

Alister
